text: Appropriate feeding during infection is essential, which demands a lot of patience from the mother. During periods of infection, children tend to eat less due to reduced appetite. Many children vomit frequently. Nutrients are also lost in urine and faeces. The unhealthy practice of restricting diet, including breast-feeding, by the mother during any sickness could further aggravate the problem. Hence, extra care is needed in feeding the child appropriately during and after illness to prevent subsequent nutritional deficiencies. Breast-feeds are often well accepted and tolerated even by sick children and should be continued except in severe gastroenteritis associated with shock.
